name: Character-Count Hash Map
summary: Bucket each word under a 26-letter frequency signature so all anagrams share one key.
visualizationIntent: init: empty hashmap of buckets, groups empty; loop(word): the current word is named in the readout, a fresh 26-slot count aux array pops in zeroed; loop(char)+update: each character bumps count[index] â€” the touched slot flashes and index marks it; update(key): count freezes into the signature key; check(key not in groups): a brand-new signature creates an empty bucket (pop-in), an existing signature highlights the matching bucket; update(append): the word chip flies into its bucket; return: every word bucketed, anagrams sharing a bucket, GROUPS holds bucket contents.

lineExplanations.3: Start with an empty map from a letter-frequency signature to the words that share it.
lineExplanations.4: Process each word one at a time.
lineExplanations.5: Reset a 26-slot tally, one slot per lowercase letter aâ€“z.
lineExplanations.6: Walk through every character of the word.
lineExplanations.7: Convert the character to a slot index: 'a' is 0, 'b' is 1, â€¦ 'z' is 25.
lineExplanations.8: Add one to that letter's slot, building the word's frequency signature.
lineExplanations.9: Freeze the 26 counts into a tuple so it can be used as a dictionary key.
lineExplanations.10: Check whether this exact signature has been seen before.
lineExplanations.11: If it is new, create an empty bucket for it.
lineExplanations.12: Drop the word into its signature's bucket â€” anagrams land together.
lineExplanations.13: Return every bucket's contents as the grouped answer.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def groupAnagrams(self, strs): the method receiving the list of words.
syntaxExplanations.4: for word in strs iterates over each string.
syntaxExplanations.5: [0] * 26 builds a list of twenty-six zeros.
syntaxExplanations.6: for char in word iterates over each character.
syntaxExplanations.7: ord(char) - ord('a') maps a letter to 0..25.
syntaxExplanations.8: count[index] += 1 adds one to that slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: tuple(count) makes an immutable copy usable as a dict key.
syntaxExplanations.10: if key not in groups tests dictionary membership.
syntaxExplanations.11: groups[key] = [] creates an empty list for the new key.
syntaxExplanations.12: groups[key].append(word) adds the word under its key.
syntaxExplanations.13: list(groups.values()) collects all buckets into one list.
